mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, it signals a severe labor market deterioration that suppresses consumer spending and corporate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and real consumer sales indicate a synchronized collapse in residential and non-residential demand, which reduces aggregate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in the index of industrial production during a financial stress regime reflects immediate supply chain disruptions and credit crunches, triggering rapid risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during financial stress periods do not yield negative 5-day returns more than 60 percent of the time, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes when controlling for volatility spikes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0 percent, it indicates aggressive central bank liquidity provision that stabilizes market funding conditions and compresses credit spreads, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain negative or flat in over 65 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS drops below 2.0 percent, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the correlation reverses during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer price inflation and falling federal funds rates creates negative real yields and erodes purchasing power, which suppresses real consumption growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between rising CPIAUCSL and falling FEDFUNDS does not predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or the signal loses significance when real yield is used directly.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between declining industrial production and stagnant GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion signals structural supply bottlenecks and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-GDPC1 divergence fails to predict negative 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) falls below 0.03 in walk-forward validation.
